summary: Public approval of Prime Minister Carney now shown at the top of the dashboard, tracked separately from the grades. Climate & Environment dropped from D+ to D. Majority government secured April 13; gas tax suspension announced April 14.

changes.text: New at the top: a public approval signal for Prime Minister Carney. It averages approve/disapprove numbers from five major Canadian pollsters (Léger, Abacus Data, Ipsos, Angus Reid Institute, Innovative Research Group) over a rolling 60-day window. It is tracked beside the grades but is explicitly not one of them — popularity is not performance.

changes.reason: Canada is on track for about half of its required 2030 emissions reduction. Industrial carbon credits are trading around $20/tonne even though the headline price is $95. Environment Canada is losing 840+ positions. The PM has acknowledged targets will be missed.

changes.text: Carney won a majority government on April 13, 2026 — 174 seats after three byelection wins.

Gas tax suspension announced April 14. The federal fuel excise is suspended from April 14 through September 7 at an estimated cost of $2.4 billion in foregone revenue. La Presse called the move "une décision insensée."
Readability pass on the dashboard: plainer card labels, shorter status lines, and a new "Limits of this model" note in the Rubric tab that explains what the scorecard can and cannot do.

summary: Climate grade revised, source stack broadened, probation dimensions (Ethics, Flagship Delivery) normalized to whole-letter C.

changes.reason: Originally recorded in the 2026-04-15 cycle; rolled forward into the 2026-04-19 publication above.

changes.text: Source stack expanded: added Canadian Climate Institute, IFSD, IRPP / Policy Options, La Presse, Toronto Star, National Observer, The Conversation Canada.
Probation dimensions (Ethics & Transparency, Flagship Delivery) now score as whole-letter C with no hidden plus/minus precision behind the displayed grade.
